item.journal.name: Fisherman's Journal
item.journal.description: A sturdy journal for tracking every fish in the valley. Hold it and right-click to learn how to use it.
message.journal-unlocked: Journal learned! Press {{key}} to open the Fisherman's Journal.
menu.journal.title: Fisherman's Journal
menu.journal.turn-page-hint: Use the arrows to browse the fish list.
config.enable-journal.name: Enable Journal
config.enable-journal.tooltip: Whether the Fisherman's Journal mechanic is enabled.
config.journal-key.name: Journal Hotkey
config.journal-key.tooltip: The key which opens the journal (after unlocking it).
menu.journal.detail.hidden-description: You haven't caught this fish yet...
menu.journal.note.title: Willy's note:
menu.journal.note.trap: Ye won't catch this one with a rod — set a crab pot and be patient.
menu.journal.diary.title: Catch Diary
menu.journal.stats.title: Statistics
menu.journal.flavor-quote: "A good catch starts with a good record." — Willy
menu.journal.note.tip.2: I've seen it at {{location}}. Cast your line {{when}}.
menu.journal.note.fact.128: The pufferfish puffs up like a balloon when scared. Don't touch the spines!
menu.journal.note.fact.129: Anchovies swim in huge schools — where there's one, there's a hundred.
menu.journal.note.fact.130: A tuna never stops swimming — even in its sleep. That's why it's all muscle.
menu.journal.note.fact.132: Bream get bolder after dark. Evening's the time for them.
menu.journal.note.fact.136: A largemouth bass can swallow prey half its own size. Greedy fella.
menu.journal.note.fact.140: Walleye eyes glow in murky water — that's how it hunts in the rain.
menu.journal.note.fact.143: A catfish's whiskers taste the water. It finds food without seeing it.
menu.journal.note.fact.145: Sunfish love warm sunlit shallows — hence the name.
menu.journal.note.fact.147: Herring find their way home across the whole sea, year after year.
menu.journal.note.fact.148: An eel can slither over wet grass from one pond to another. Seen it myself!
menu.journal.note.fact.151: A squid has three hearts, and its blood is blue. Strange creatures of the deep...
menu.journal.note.fact.164: The sandfish 'swims' through desert sand like it's water.
menu.journal.note.fact.159: The Crimsonfish... my old rival. Legends say it's been circling the east pier for fifty years.
menu.journal.note.fact.160: The Angler lurks north of town. Only a true master gets that one on a hook.
menu.journal.note.fact.163: The Legend. One fisherman in a generation lands it. Maybe it'll be you.
menu.journal.note.fact.682: The Mutant Carp... something in that sewer water changed it forever.
menu.journal.note.fact.775: The Glacierfish hides at the icy tip of Arrowhead Island. Dress warm.
menu.journal.pond.title: Fish Pond
menu.journal.pond.spawn-daily: Breeding: a new fish every day
menu.journal.pond.spawn-days: Breeding: a new fish every {{days}} days
menu.journal.pond.no-gates: The pond grows on its own, no requests needed.
menu.journal.pond.produce-note: The pond is checked every morning — the more fish in it, the better the chance of finding something.
